title: Erdős Distinct Subset Sums Conjecture
statement_text: If $\{a_1, \ldots, a_n\}$ is a set of positive integers with $a_1 < \cdots < a_n$ and all subset sums distinct, then $a_n \geq c \cdot 2^n$ for some constant $c > 0$.
context_text: Erdős conjectured this and offered $500 for a proof or disproof. Erdős and Moser proved $a_n \geq \frac{1}{4} \cdot n^{-1/2} \cdot 2^n$ using the second moment method; subsequent improvements on the constant factor were made by Guy, Elkies, Bae, and Aliev, with the best published constant being $\sqrt{3/2\pi}$ due to Aliev.
paper_contribution: Gives two short proofs of the best known asymptotic lower bound $a_n \geq \left(\sqrt{2/\pi} - o(1)\right) \cdot n^{-1/2} \cdot 2^n$, matching an unpublished result of Elkies and Gleason; the first proof combines the Berry–Esseen theorem with Moser's second-moment argument, and the second uses Harper's vertex-isoperimetric inequality.
notes: PDF source — math may be garbled. The conjecture statement is rendered clearly in context but is stated informally in prose rather than in a labelled theorem environment.
paper_title: A note on the Erdős distinct subset sums problem
